label: Recurrence & feedback state

node.label: Recurrence & feedback state
node.definition: A state is repeatedly updated as inputs arrive, enabling temporal dependence and iterative computation.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Recurrence & feedback state is modeled as depending on Sequences, events & time series, Gating & multiplicative control and feeding downstream nodes such as Differentiable memory read/write, Recurrent neural networks, Energy-based, Hopfield & Boltzmann models.
examples: atlas downstream link to Differentiable memory read/write
atlas downstream link to Recurrent neural networks
atlas downstream link to Energy-based, Hopfield & Boltzmann models
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Sequences, events & time series, Gating & multiplicative control.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Recurrence & feedback state has a `uses` dependency on Sequences, events & time series because Sequences, events & time series is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
Recurrence & feedback state is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Gating & multiplicative control.

In this atlas graph, Recurrence & feedback state has a `uses` dependency on Dynamical systems & control theory because Dynamical systems & control theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
